item.title: More substance is needed
item.content:encoded:
I have upvoted this article because one, I like how its a homage to the secret oddity van from Amazing World of Gumball and two, its just a interesting concept.

Please if you could add more items to what has been found inside scp 2538. I'd love to see this concept more fully explored and revised.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The audio recording does not sound natural at all. I cringe after reading the second record.

The whole article does not tell anything interesting at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted, it just doesn't carry itself well, and all those references were pretty overboard. PM me if this is rewritten so I can look at it again.

item.title: Going overboard with references
item.content:encoded:
I have to say, as a gamer who had played a lot of PAYDAY 2 in the past, trying to enjoy this article is incredibly difficult. The references got so overboard they ended up distracting me from the story itself.

From the van itself, to the heist equipment (special mention goes to the wacky masks), to the 4-man gang, to a man named Dragan being involved in this situation, and the cheesy phone recordings discussing the progress of the jobs. Everything here is way too similar to be a mere coincidence. To add icing on the cake, there is an exact song in PAYDAY 2 called "Where's the Van?!".

What are references to other media for? Mostly as small gags, irrelevant to the larger situation or covert enough to be overlooked. But filling up an entire scene with overt references and still trying to tell a serious story (without using the skill of Lampshade Hanging to somehow remedy the suspension of disbelief) behind it all is not an effective method at all, as it shatters the audience's suspension of disbelief, making them unable to properly enjoy the work.

If the author was attempting to make a gag-based comedy where suspension of disbelief is not important, such references are fine. But this SCP doesn't seem like a comedy at all, and feels more like a classical SCP story about implications to the larger picture. The references ended up dragging the whole thing underwater.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guess it wasn't Payday for Dragan and Marco.

Shame, really. Dragan was second only to Hoxton.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The end of this tale I think helps your point

item.title: A notice
item.content:encoded:
Due to the amount of negative feedback I will might be rewriting this scip in the future. Most likely after I've already gotten some more articles on the site. Again, thanks to everyone who took the time to read this.

Edit: I didn't want to make any promises so I changed the wording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree with Jacob and Kalinin; the van is taking the backseat here. Im also getting the notion that MCD is becoming (or already is) the Slenderman of this site. Regardless, I thought the story that unfolded was fairly decent but there needs to be a better power balance between the story and the scip itself.

Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The MC&D reference really feels shoehorned in on this one, and the dialogue in the calls is particularly weighed down by exposition. The idea of petty criminals making their own paratech and using it for crimes is something that I can get behind, but this doesn't do enough with the idea for my to upvote.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The final notes are far too "explain the concept". And what kind of van is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reads to me like a basic, fairly uninteresting idea (magic crime van) with a half-hearted attempt at a backstory stapled onto it. I feel like either the item involved in the article should be interesting enough that its existence challenges previously-held ideas about reality in such a way that it's interesting on its own, or it plays an integral part in a compelling story of some kind.

This story isn't even really developed. It's a mysterious organization trying to steal some dude's magic van. That's pretty much it. And it's full of stuff like this:

With the success of this arms deal, I'm happy to welcome you as one of our traffickers.

I'm fairly certain that successful criminals don't generally make a habit of leaving recorded voicemails on peoples' phones using the literal terms "arms deal" and "trafficker." Very little about any of this made much sense, and if you're relying on a backstory to carry the article, it's going to need to be a lot better than what's being offered here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ya know, its amazing how much of difference one little addition can make: that last phone call from Dragan makes it all come together, without any added flash. Nice work, and +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I said it already but it appears a simple story works out in this case.

Also, thank you for contributing to my challenge. Thought it would have sunk in the depths of the forum.

And yes, given that the Balkans is at the intersection of so many historic powers, it is absurd that there is less SCPs in those regions.

item.title: "Where's the van!"
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Quin, MrWrong, Scorpion451, and Steeltitan for feedback. This is being submitted under Mr. Wrong's Fill up the SCP map challenge , as an entry for my homeland of Serbia.

This is my 2nd SCP, so let's hope I learned a lesson from my first one.
